title: Paspalum glaziovii
taxonomy: Paspalum glaziovii is classified as a type of grass within the family Poaceae and is further categorized as part of the tribe Paniceae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other grass species. intro: Paspalum glaziovii is a tropical grass species native to South America, commonly known as Glaziou's paspalum. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
